title: Chudnovsky–Seymour Coarse Erdős–Pósa Conjecture
statement_text: There exist functions $f,\,g:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that for all integers $k\geq 1$ and $d\geq 1$, for every graph $G$, either $G$ contains $k$ cycles where the distance between each pair of cycles is greater than $d$, or there exists a subset $X$ of vertices of $G$ with $|X|\leq f(k)$ such that $G-B_{G}(X,g(d))$ is a forest.
context_text: Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu proposed a research theme combining graph minors and coarse geometry, including the Coarse Erdős–Pósa statement [3, Conjecture 9.7]. The specific variant above was conjectured by Chudnovsky and Seymour (discussed at the Barbados Graph Theory Workshop, March 2024) and independently posed as a conjecture by Ahn et al. [1], who solved the cases $k=2$ with arbitrary $d$, and $d=1$ with arbitrary $k$.
attributed_to: Chudnovsky and Seymour; also Ahn, Gollin, Huynh, and Kwon
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality with explicit bounds $f(k)\in\mathcal{O}(k^{18}\operatorname{polylog}k)$ and $g(d)=19d$.
notes: Attribution to Chudnovsky and Seymour is via a footnote referencing the Barbados workshop (March 2024), not a formal citation. Ahn et al. [1] (SODA 2025) independently posed the same conjecture.
paper_title: Erdős--Pósa property of cycles that are far apart
